Cannes: Exiled Russian filmmaker Andrey Zvyagintsev's 'Minotaur' delivers anti-war message through intimate lens
subhead: The 62-year-old filmmaker, now living in France, chose the conflict between his country and Ukraine as the backdrop for his latest film. He depicts the consequences of the war, which he openly condemns, within the realm of married life. Despite his open hatred of Putin's regime, his mere presence at Cannes could draw condemnation from Ukrainian advocacy groups.

title: 'Macron is paving the way for the return of African and Asian artifacts from French museums'
subhead: The unanimous adoption by France's parliament in early May of the law on the restitution of cultural property to foreign countries is a remarkable achievement in times of identity tensions, says Michel Guerrin, editor-in-chief at Le Monde, in his column.

title: Elon Musk criticizes Christopher Nolan for casting Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy
subhead: The billionaire accused the filmmaker of 'defiling Homer' by choosing a Black actress, claiming Nolan only did so to comply with diversity requirements and win an Oscar. The controversy echoes the Trump administration's opposition to affirmative action policies.

title: Inside a Texas migrant detention center: 'They treat us like animals'
subhead: At the border between the US and Mexico, federal immigration authorities have opened a massive detention site with 5,000 beds on the military grounds of Fort Bliss. A former detainee recounts the hell she endured behind those walls.
